---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
library(brms)
library(tidyverse)
library(BayesFactor)
library(BayesianId)
```

```{r}
data("penguins")
test_data = na.omit(penguins) %>% rename(y = body_mass, x1 = flipper_len, x2 = bill_len, f1 = island, f2 = sex)
test_data = test_data |> dplyr::mutate(y_bin = test_data$f2, y_cens = ifelse(y > 4800, 4800, y))

levels(test_data$f1) |> print()
levels(test_data$f2) |> print()
nrow(test_data) |> print()
```

```{r}
test_data[10:20,]
```

```{r}
fit = breg(y ~ x1, 
           data = test_data |> dplyr::filter(f1 == "Torgersen"))
```

```{r}
fit = breg(#y ~ x1 + x2,
           y_bin ~ x1 + x2,
           data = test_data,
           #family = "normal_linear",
           family = "bernoulli_logistic",
           prior_scale = 10,
           center = TRUE,
           iter = 10000,
           warmup = 1000)
coef(fit)
```

```{r}
foo = make_stan_data(formula = y_bin ~ 1,
           data = test_data,
           #family = "normal_linear",
           family = "bernoulli_logistic",
           prior_scale = 10,
           center = TRUE)
foo$Y[1:5]
```


```{r}
plot(fit, type = "post_pred")
```

```{r}
plot(fit, type = "trace")
```


```{r}
foo = posterior_predict(fit)
```

```{r}
gfit = glm(#y ~ x1 + x2, 
           y_bin ~ x1 + x2,
           data = test_data, 
           #family = gaussian()
           family = binomial()
           )
coef(gfit)
```
